LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAUL PELOSI'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. "A PLEA IN ABATEMENT PURSUANT TO SECTION 430.10, SUBDIVISION (C), MAY BE MADE BY DEMURRER OR ANSWER WHEN THERE IS ANOTHER ACTION PENDING BETWEEN THE SAME PARTIES ON THE SAME CAUSE OF ACTION. [CITATIONS] IN DETERMINING WHETHER THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE THE SAME FOR PURPOSES OF PLEAS IN ABATEMENT, THE RULE IS THAT SUCH A PLEA MAY BE MAINTAINED ONLY WHERE A JUDGMENT IN THE FIRST ACTION WOULD BE A COMPLETE BAR TO THE SECOND ACTION." PLANT INSULATION CO. V. FIBREBOARD CORP. (1990) 224 CAL.APP.3D 781, 787-788. IN THIS CASE, THE APPELLATE COURT'S DECISION IN GREEN I WILL NOT HAVE A RES JUDICATA EFFECT ON GREEN II. GREEN I ADJUDICATED PLAINTIFF'S RIGHTS WITH REGARD TO THE EXPRESS WRITTEN AGREEMENT WITH THE UFL. GREEN II RELATES TO A SEPARATE ORAL GUARANTEE THAT IMPLICATES A DIFFERENT PRIMARY RIGHT. GREEN I NEVER ADJUDICATED THE ALLEGED ORAL AGREEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302: DEFENDANT WILLIAM HAMBRECHT'S ("HAMBRECHT") MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE TWO YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OF CCP 339 APPLIES TO PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF ORAL CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE THREE YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OF CCP 338 APPLIES TO PLAINTIFF'S FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION. IT IS CLEAR THAT DENNIS GREEN ("GREEN") KNEW OF HIS EMPLOYER UNITED FOOTBALL LEAGUE, LLC'S ("UFL") DEFAULT BY OCTOBER 7, 2011 WHEN HE COMMENCED GREEN I. GREEN'S CLAIMS AGAINST HAMBRECHT ACCRUED BY THAT TIME. "THE GENERAL RULE IS THAT THE LIABILITY OF A SURETY ACCRUES AT THE SAME TIME AS THAT OF THE PRINCIPAL. AND A CAUSE OF ACTION UPON A BOND CONDITIONED TO DO A CERTAIN ACT ACCRUES AS SOON AS THERE IS A DEFAULT IN PERFORMANCE." (BROCK V. WESTERN NAT'L INDEMN. CO. (1955) 132 CAL.APP.2D 10, 16; SEE ALSO CAL. CIVIL CODE SEC. 2807; CAL. FIRST BANK V. BRADEN (1989) 216 CAL.APP.3D 672, 677.) GREEN FILED THIS UNTIMELY ACTION ON SEPTEMBER 3, 2015. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES). ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dan Siegel, Esq. 510-839-1200 of Siegel Yee & Brunner for Plaintiff Dennis Green.
Alison Maddeford, Esq. 415-275-8550 of Riley Safer Holmes & Cancila LLP for Defendant WILLIAM HAMBRECHT.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILLIAM HAMBRECHT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

Defendant William Hambrecht's ("Hambrecht") motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication as to the third amended complaint is DENIED in its entirety. The two year statute of limitations of CCP 339 applies to plaintiff's breach of oral contract cause of action and the three year statute of limitations of CCP 338 applies to plaintiff's fraud causes of action. It is clear that Dennis Green ("Green") knew of his employer United Football League, LLC's ("UFL") default by October 7, 2011 when he commenced Green I. Green's claims against Hambrecht accrued by that time. "The general rule is that the liability of a surety accrues at the same time as that of the principal. And a cause of action upon a bond conditioned to do a certain act accrues as soon as there is a default in performance." (Brock v. Western Nat'l Indemn. Co. (1955) 132 Cal.App.2d 10, 16; see also Cal. Civil Code sec. 2807; Cal. First Bank v. Braden (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 672, 677.) Green filed this untimely action on September 3, 2015. (see Word minutes for full details)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WILLIAM HAMBRECHT AMENDED DEMURRER to all three causes of action in the 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT filed by plaintiff Marie Green is overruled. Ms. Green alleges sufficient facts to invoke the doctrine of equitable tolling of the statute of limitations as to all three claims. A plaintiff seeking to invoke equitable tolling must show that: 1) the prior action gave timely notice to the defendant, 2) lack of prejudice to the defendant; and 3) reasonable and good faith conduct by her. (Hopkins v. Kedzierski (2014) 225 Cal.App.4th 736, 747). Ms. Green alleges sufficient facts to satisfy the timely notice requirement. In Green I Mr. Green sought to recover against the UFL for breach of his written employment contract and for fraud. While Mr. Hambrecht was not a named defendant in Green I, he participated in that case and numerous paragraphs of the complaint in Green I allege that Mr. Hambrecht made representations that he would fund the UFL and Mr. Green's salary. (Green I complaint pars. 15, 18, 19, 32, 35.) Those representations form the basis of Green II. Mr. Hambrecht did not need to be a party to Green I for equitable tolling to apply. (Id. at 750-751.) Mr. Green's previous admission that Green I and Green II involve different claims does not preclude application of the equitable tolling doctrine. The court cannot presently determine as a matter of law that Mr. Hambrecht has been prejudiced by the death of Mr. Green. Paragraphs 47-49 of the third amended complaint, which the court accepts as true on demurrer, show that the delay has not caused prejudice. Insofar as prejudice is concerned, the critical distinction between Mr. Hambrecht and Mr. Pelosi is that Ms. Green has alleged in the third amended complaint that the complaint and discovery in Green I made Mr. Hambrecht aware of the allegations that lie at the heart of Green II. Ms. Green alleges sufficient facts to show good faith and reasonable conduct. While Mr. Green could have concurrently pursued claims against the UFL and Mr. Hambrecht, equitable tolling is not to be determined retrospectively based on the success or failure of the plaintiff pursuing an alternative remedy. (Id. at 749.) Paragraphs 33-34 and 50-51of the third amended complaint sufficiently allege facts showing good faith and reasonable conduct because Mr. Green believed that he would have been made whole by Green I. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dan Siegel of Siegel & Yee telephone number 510-839-1200 for Plaintiff Dennis Green.
Steve Hankins of Riley Safer Holmes & Cancila LLP for Defendant William Hambrecht.
Ryan Polk of Gordon & Rees telephone number 510-837-8470 for Defendant Paul Pelosi.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling.
Defendant William Hambrecht's demurrer to all three causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with 20-days leave to amend to allege, if Mr. Green can do so in good, facts supporting equitable tolling. All three claims are based on numerous statements allegedly constituting promises that were made over several years that defendants would guarantee plaintiff's salary. The first group of promises was made in 2009 - 2011. Those promises are time-barred under the three year statute of limitations governing fraud and Labor Code 970 and the two year statute of limitations governing oral contracts because Mr. Green knew that he had not been paid all of his salary by October 2011 when he filed Green I. "The general rule is that the liability of a surety accrues at the same time as that of the principal. And a cause of action upon a bond conditioned to do a certain act accrues as soon as there is a default in performance." (Brock v. Western Nat'l Indemn. Co. (1955) 132 Cal.App.2d 10, 16; see also Civil Code 2807 (surety liable upon principal's default)). The second group of promises was made in October 2012 - July 2013. Mr. Green alleges that he detrimentally relied on those promises by continuing to pursue his salary claim in Green I. Apart from the statement attributed to Mr. Hambrecht during that time period which is not actionable due to the litigation privilege, none of those alleged promises can fairly be construed as stating that defendants would pay an arbitration award. Even if any of the second group of promises could be so construed, Mr. Green's reliance on such promises is not reasonable as a matter of law. By October 2012 Mr. Green knew that the defendants had refused to pay all the salary that Mr. Green claimed was owed to him, notwithstanding the group of promises allegedly made in 2009-2011. Under these circumstances nothing in the second group of promises would lead a reasonable person to believe that defendants would pay the arbitration award. Moreover, Mr. Green's continued pursuit of Green I is entirely explained by his desire to obtain an arbitration award against the respondent in the arbitration proceedings and to use the award as a basis for his later unsuccessful effort to add defendants to the judgment confirming the award. Mr. Green did not alter his conduct based on any of the alleged promises made after the filing of Green I. A plaintiff establishes reliance "when the misrepresentation ' was an immediate cause of the plaintiff's conduct which altered his or her legal relations, and when without such misrepresentation or nondisclosure he or she would not, in all reasonable probability, have entered into the contract or other transaction." (Alliance Mortgage Co. v. Rothwell, 10 Cal.4th 1226, 1239). The oral contract cause of action also accrued by October 2011 when Mr. Green filed Green I. Mr. Green does not allege any oral statement where defendants expressly agreed to pay the arbitration award or a judgment confirming it. Even if Mr. Hambrecht's statement during the arbitration was such a statement, the litigation privilege precludes liability. Moreover, an agreement to pay the award/judgment is distinct from the alleged oral agreement to pay the wages and new consideration is required, and none is alleged. The parties' requests for judicial notice are granted. Counsel for defendant is to prepare a form of order and present it to Mr. Siegel.
Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jalle Dafa of Siegel & Yee telephone number 510-839-1200 for Plaintiff.
Stuart Gordon and Ryan Polk of Gordon & Rees telephone number 415-986-5900 for Defendant Paul Pelosi.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PAUL PELOSI'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is overruled. "A plea in abatement pursuant to section 430.10, subdivision (c), may be made by demurrer or answer when there is another action pending between the same parties on the same cause of action. [citations] In determining whether the causes of action are the same for purposes of pleas in abatement, the rule is that such a plea may be maintained only where a judgment in the first action would be a complete bar to the second action." Plant Insulation Co. v. Fibreboard Corp. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 781, 787-788. In this case, the appellate court's decision in Green I will not have a res judicata effect on Green II. Green I adjudicated plaintiff's rights with regard to the express written agreement with the UFL. Green II relates to a separate oral guarantee that implicates a different primary right. Green I never adjudicated the alleged oral agreement. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
